user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Tailspin Toys. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 16:47 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 08:42 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Tailspin Toys

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 16:59 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys account health

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:08:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:10:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:22:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-F trap (Skips prior transcript when one exists for the series); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-F failure mode (Skips prior transcript when one exists for the series)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
